---
title: "Submission template"
subtitle: "Everything participating teams need lives in one clone-able repository"
toc: false
css: tournament.css
---

All working materials — the survey, the codebook, example submissions, the reporting checklist, and
the scripts that clean and self-check a submission — live in a dedicated GitHub repository that **is**
a submission template:

[**github.com/janpfander/silicon-sample-submission**](https://github.com/janpfander/silicon-sample-submission){.btn .btn-primary}

Clone it (or click **“Use this template”**), drop in your predictions, and release it to Zenodo —
full step-by-step instructions are in the repository's `README`. It ships with a random-placeholder
example so a fresh clone already passes the self-check, showing you the exact target structure.

The **submission file schemas** and the **human target sample** (recruitment, census-based quotas, and
the quota table) are specified in the **[benchmark amendment](amendment_preregistration.qmd)**.
